sources: AS/NZS 3017:2022 — Electrical installations — Verification by inspection and testing
AS/NZS 3012:2019 (inc Amd 001) — Electrical installations — Construction and demolition sites
AS/NZS 3760:2022 (inc Amd No. 1) — In-service safety inspection and testing of electrical equipment and RCDs
metadata.description: Structured clause knowledge base for auxiliary standards

AS_NZS_3017_2022.title: Electrical installations — Verification by inspection and testing
AS_NZS_3017_2022.scope: Sets out common inspection and test methods to verify low voltage electrical installations comply with safety requirements. Covers MEN (TN-C-S) systems.

4_1.title: Competent person
4_1.description: Person who has acquired knowledge and skill through education, training, qualification or experience to perform the required task.

4_3.title: Earth fault-loop impedance
4_3.description: Impedance of the earth fault-current loop (active-to-earth loop) starting and ending at the point of earth fault.

4_11.title: Known earth
4_11.description: Earth point previously tested to confirm it is connected to the installation earthing system.

4_16.title: Verification
4_16.description: Provision of objective evidence that a given item fulfils specified requirements. Comprises inspection, testing and reporting.

5_1.title: Safety
5_1.description: Testing inherently involves hazard and shall be undertaken by a competent person. Site-specific risk assessment required before commencing.

2_1.title: Equipment required
2_1.description: Voltage indicator, volt meter, low range ohmmeter, insulation resistance test instrument, EFLI measuring instrument, RCD test instrument/device.

2_2_1_2.title: Meter error
2_2_1_2.description: Max inherent error: (a) analogue — 5% full scale deflection; (b) digital — 5% of value being measured.

4_3.title: Test for de-energized
4_3.description: All circuits shall be proven de-energized and isolated before testing.

4_4_earth_continuity.title: Earth continuity and resistance
4_4_earth_continuity.description: Testing to prove continuity of earthing system: main earthing conductor, protective earthing conductors, bonding conductors.

pass_fail.main_earthing_conductor: Resistance shall be not more than 0.5 Ohm
pass_fail.equipotential_bonding_conductor: Resistance shall be not more than 0.5 Ohm
pass_fail.protective_earthing_conductor: Low enough to permit passage of current to operate overcurrent protective device; consistent with length, CSA and material type.
pass_fail.method: Disconnect main earthing conductor, measure between conductor and earth electrode with low-range ohmmeter.

4_5_insulation_resistance.title: Insulation resistance
4_5_insulation_resistance.description: Ensures insulation between all live conductors and earth is adequate to prevent shock, fire and equipment damage.
4_5_insulation_resistance.method: Apply DC 500 V for LV circuits. Disconnect neutral from MEN system. Remove MEN link.

pass_fail.rcd_functional_earth: Not less than 0.05 MOhm (or as per manufacturer)
pass_fail.reduced_voltage_test: At 250 V d.c. if sensitive equipment connected, minimum still 1 MOhm

4_6_polarity.title: Polarity and correct circuit connections
4_6_polarity.description: Ensures no shock hazard from incorrect connection of active, neutral and earthing conductors.
requirements: Every single-pole switch or protective device shall operate in the active conductor
Multi-phase switch/protective device shall operate in all active conductors
RCDs required to switch all live conductors shall switch active and neutral
Socket-outlets for flat-pin plugs: order earth, active, neutral clockwise from front
All neutral conductors connected to neutral bar of switchboard
Consumers mains neutral connected to neutral bar of main switchboard
No short-circuit between conductors, no transposition, no interconnection between different circuits
4_6_polarity.verification: Polarity verified by approximate zero-Ohm reading on each conductor; infinity reading on incorrect configurations.

4_7_phase_sequence.title: Phase sequence of multi-phase socket outlets
4_7_phase_sequence.description: Where multi-phase socket-outlets of the same type are installed, phase sequence shall be the same throughout.

4_8_earth_fault_loop_impedance.title: Earth fault-loop impedance (EFLI)
4_8_earth_fault_loop_impedance.description: Measured to ensure sufficient fault current to operate protective device within specified disconnection time.

method.energized: Use EFLI tester at furthest point of each branch of final subcircuit. MEN left intact.
method.de_energized: Measure total resistance (Rphe) of active + protective earthing conductors using ohmmeter.

4_8_earth_fault_loop_impedance.pass_fail: Maximum EFLI shall not exceed values from Table 4.5 (MCBs: Type B/C/D) or Ia per manufacturer data. For 0.4s disconnection: e.g., 6A MCB Type B = 9.6 Ohm, Type C = 5.1 Ohm; 20A Type B = 2.9 Ohm, Type C = 1.5 Ohm.

4_9_rcd_testing.title: Confirming function of residual current devices (RCDs)
4_9_rcd_testing.description: Ensures RCD correctly installed, operates at approximately rated residual current, and disconnects all required conductors.
methods: Integral test button (push-button) — device must activate without undue delay
RCD test instrument at socket outlet — verify correct operation at rated I_delta_n
pass_fail: RCD operates when residual current of approximately rated value is present
Protected circuit(s) disconnected from supply to required degree (all actives, or all actives plus neutral)

4_10_earth_electrode.title: Measuring resistance of earth electrode to general mass of earth
4_10_earth_electrode.description: Tests integrity of the earth electrode system.

4_11_touch_voltage.title: Measuring touch voltage
4_11_touch_voltage.description: Procedure for measuring touch voltage at electrical installations.

4_12_incoming_neutral.title: Test for integrity of the incoming neutral
4_12_incoming_neutral.description: Tests that the incoming neutral conductor is continuous and correctly connected.

AS_NZS_3012_2019.title: Electrical installations — Construction and demolition sites (inc Amd 001)
AS_NZS_3012_2019.scope: Minimum requirements for design, construction and testing of electrical installations supplying appliances and equipment on construction and demolition sites, and in-service testing of electrical equipment used on such sites.

2_4_6_3.title: RCD protection on construction sites
2_4_6_3.description: All socket-outlets shall be protected by RCD with max rated residual current of 30 mA operating in all live conductors (active and neutral). In NZ, Type A RCD per IEC 61008-1 or IEC 61009-1.

2_6.title: Flexible cords, cord extension sets, flexible cables and accessories
2_6.description: Requirements for flexible cords and cables on construction sites.

2_7_3_1.title: Emergency lighting
2_7_3_1.description: Requirement to prevent unauthorised disconnection of emergency lighting.

2_7_6.title: Temporary protected lighting (TPL)
2_7_6.description: TPL per AS/NZS 60598.2.20 not considered festoon lighting.

3_1.title: Application
3_1.description: Describes verification (assessment, inspection and testing) procedures and frequency for all components of electrical installations on construction and demolition sites.

3_2_frequency.title: Frequency of verification
3_2_frequency.description: Determined by hazard/risk level and degree of misuse based on specific conditions, not general site conditions.

requirements.new_equipment: Prior to initial introduction into service
requirements.repaired_equipment: Prior to return to service after repair or servicing affecting electrical safety
requirements.hire_equipment: Inspection prior to each hire, testing at not greater than monthly intervals
requirements.all_equipment: Intervals not exceeding those in Table 6

rcd_pushbutton.portable: After connection to a socket or before connection of equipment, and at least once every day in use

3_3.title: Construction wiring and transportable structures
3_3.description: All construction wiring, including switchboards, fixed RCDs and transportable structures, shall be inspected and tested by a qualified person following initial installation and periodically per Table 6.

3_4_rcds.title: RCDs
3_4_rcds.description: RCDs shall be (a) successfully operated by in-built test facility (push-button), (b) subject to and comply with operating time test per AS/NZS 3760, and (c) tested by a competent person. In NZ, check RCD labelled as Type A per IEC 61008-1 or IEC 61009-1.

3_5.title: Other electrical equipment on site
3_5.description: Power tools, flexible cords, cord extension sets and PSOAs shall be tested before being put into service and at intervals per Table 6.
3_5.protective_earthing_continuity: Class I equipment: continuity from plug earth pin to exposed conductive parts per AS/NZS 3760. Resistance less than max values in AS/NZS 3760 (1 Ohm per AS 3760 Clause 2.4.3.1).
3_5.insulation_resistance: Class I and Class II: tested per AS/NZS 3760. Values not less than minimum in AS/NZS 3760 (1.0 MOhm generally).
3_5.arc_welders_table_7: Input circuit to welding circuit: 5 MOhm min. All circuits to exposed conductive parts: 2.5 MOhm min. Welding circuit to auxiliary circuits > ELV: 1 MOhm min.

3_6.title: Generator connections
3_6.description: RCD-equipped generators: verify integrity of connection between windings and equipotential bonding. Isolated winding generators: frame-to-bonding connection < 1 Ohm; winding-to-frame insulation > 1 MOhm.

3_7.title: Actions from inspection and test
3_7.description: Non-compliant equipment: withdraw from service immediately, attach out-of-service tag, send for repair/disposal/destruction. Compliant equipment: fit durable non-reusable non-metallic tag with person's name, test and re-test dates.
3_7.tag_colour_coding: Refer to Appendix D for recommended colour coding schedule. Tags identify period in which test was performed.

3_9.title: Documentation
3_9.description: Records of inspection and testing to be kept.

d.title: Appendix D — Recommended colours for tags on tested equipment
d.description: Colour coding schedule for test tags.

e.title: Appendix E — Electrical verification of generators with RCD protection
e.description: Test procedures for generators per AS/NZS 3012 and AS/NZS 3760.

f.title: Appendix F — Electrical verification of portable inverters

j.title: Appendix J — Construction power supply arrangements for domestic housing sites (NZ only)
j.description: New Zealand-specific requirements for domestic housing site power.

108.title: Application of the Australian/New Zealand Wiring Rules
108.description: AS/NZS 3000 applied with modifications: (a) 'regulatory authority' = Energy Safe Victoria; (b) 'electrician' = licensed electrician; (c) 'licensed electrical contractor' = registered electrical contractor; (d) water/gas suppliers includes telecom suppliers.

202.title: General wiring methods
202.description: Installations must comply with Divisions 1-9 of Part 2 and Part 2 of AS/NZS 3000.

214.title: Electricity supplies — construction and demolition sites
214.description: (1) Switchboards/enclosures on C&D sites must be securely attached to rigid structure. (2) Free-standing enclosures must be securely fixed. (3) Underground consumer's mains entering switchboard must be enclosed in heavy duty non-metallic conduit from ground exit to enclosure, with additional mechanical protection (WSX3 per AS/NZS 3013).

240.title: General testing requirements for LV electrical installation work
240.description: LV electrical installation work must be tested per AS/NZS 3000 to verify compliance with Divisions 1-9 of Part 2 after completion and before certification or inspection.

241.title: Testing of underground consumer's mains
241.description: New underground consumer's mains: insulation resistance tested per Reg 228(1) after completion. Existing mains after work: tested per Reg 228(2).

242.title: Testing in patient areas
242.description: Must be tested per AS/NZS 3000 to verify compliance with AS/NZS 3003.

243.title: Testing of photovoltaic arrays
243.description: Must be tested per AS/NZS 5033 to verify compliance. Penalty: 20 penalty units.

244.title: Testing of battery systems
244.description: Must be tested per AS/NZS 3000 to verify compliance with AS/NZS 5139. Penalty: 20 penalty units.

245.title: Testing of grid-connected inverter energy systems
245.description: After completion and before service: (a) inverter takes >60s to connect to network after main switch closed; (b) inverter disconnects from network in <2s after main switch opened. Penalty: 20 penalty units.

246.title: Testing of HV electrical installation work
246.description: HV installations tested per AS 2067. Earthing systems: resistance to earth not greater than design, connections maintain conductivity, earth potential rise does not exceed design.

247.title: Persons authorised to carry out testing
247.description: Testing must be carried out by: (a) a licensed electrician; (b) a person licensed to carry out the work; or (c) for HV work, a competent person.

248.title: Results of HV testing
248.description: Person carrying out HV testing must: (a) record results in writing; (b) give copy to premises owner; (c) retain copy for 3 years. Penalty: 20 penalty units.

249.title: Electrical installation work that must be inspected
249.description: Prescribed electrical installation work includes: consumer's mains, main earthing systems, consumer's terminals, connection devices, overhead service line supports, overcurrent protective devices at origin of private aerial lines, main switchboard parts related to control or fire protection.

250.title: Inspection of prescribed electrical installation work
250.description: Prescribed work must be inspected by a licensed electrical inspector.

251.title: Certificates of inspection
251.description: Certificates must be issued for prescribed installation work.

AS_NZS_3760_2022.title: In-service safety inspection and testing of electrical equipment and RCDs (inc Amd No. 1)
AS_NZS_3760_2022.scope: Procedures for safety inspection and testing of low voltage single phase and polyphase electrical equipment connected by flexible cord or connecting device. Covers new, in-service, repaired, second-hand, and hire equipment. Also covers RCDs (Australia) and portable inverters.

1_1.title: Scope
1_1.description: Applies to portable, hand-held, stationary equipment; cord sets, cord extension sets, EPODs; PRCDs, ACS, PSOAs; flexible cords in hostile environments; portable power supplies; battery chargers; heavy duty tools. Does NOT apply to equipment at height >=2.5m where no reasonable chance of touching earth.

1_2_4_2.title: Hire equipment responsibility
1_2_4_2.description: Responsibility for testing, inspection and tagging passes to hiree. Retest interval derived from Table 2.4.

2_2_frequency.title: Frequency of inspection and tests
2_2_frequency.description: Equipment inspected and tested at intervals indicated in Table 2.4 (tolerance 2 weeks) or as varied by documented risk assessment. Return to service after repair (AS/NZS 5762). Second-hand before service (AS/NZS 5761).
2_2_frequency.risk_assessment: Organizations may substitute other periods after documented risk assessment per AS/NZS ISO 31000. This option does NOT apply to hire equipment.

2_3_personnel.title: Competent person
2_3_personnel.description: Tasks carried out by a competent person with understanding of Class I/II equipment, insulation types, protective earth, earth continuity, insulation resistance, leakage current, relevant legislation.

2_4_1.title: Inspection and testing includes
2_4_1.description: (a) External inspection; (b) Protective earth continuity tests for Class I, EPODs, cord sets, cord extension sets; (c) Insulation testing (IR or leakage current); (d) Polarity of rewireable plugs/sockets.

2_4_2_inspection.title: Visual and physical inspection
2_4_2_inspection.description: Check for: damage/defects/modifications, cord anchoring, cord damage (inner cores not exposed, sheath not cut/abraded/twisted), EPOD load warning label, operating controls, covers/guards, ventilation, plug pin damage, cord extension socket shroud, current rating consistency.

2_4_3_1_earth_continuity.title: Earthing continuity
2_4_3_1_earth_continuity.description: Continuity from plug earth pin to accessible earthed parts (Class I) and to earth contact of each outlet (cord sets, EPODs, PRCDs).

2_4_3_2_insulation_testing.title: Testing of insulation
2_4_3_2_insulation_testing.description: Leakage current test OR insulation resistance test per Appendix E.

rows.method: Current between accessible metal and earth

rows.equipment: Cord extension sets, cord sets, EPODs, PRCDs

rows.method: Between live parts and accessible earthed parts
Between live parts and any accessible metal parts
Between live parts and functional earthing conductor

rows.equipment: Cord extension sets, cord sets, EPODs, PRCDs
rows.method: Between live parts and protective earthing conductor

rows.equipment: Class I with mineral insulated metal sheath heating elements
rows.method: Between live parts and accessible earthed parts

table_2_3_rcd_tripping.title: Table 2.3 — Maximum tripping times (RCDs)
notes: RCDs permanently wired: test button only, observe 'without undue delay'. In doubt, use external timing: 30mA RCD max 300ms, 10mA RCD max 40ms.
Push-button test only confirms operation, not actual trip-time.

table_2_4_inspection_intervals.title: Table 2.4 — Indicative testing and inspection intervals

rows.rcd_pushbutton_portable: Daily or before every use, whichever longer

rows.environment: Environment where equipment/cord subject to flexing OR open to abuse OR hostile environment
Environment where equipment/cord NOT subject to flexing AND NOT open to abuse AND NOT hostile
Residential type areas (hotels, motels, boarding houses, halls, hostels)

rows.rcd_pushbutton_portable: Daily or before every use, whichever longer

rows.environment: Hire equipment — inspection, test and tag
rows.rcd_pushbutton_portable: Including push-button test by hirer prior to hire

rows.equipment_interval: After repair or service that could affect electrical safety, or on reintroduction to service. AS/NZS 5762 applies.

2_5_1_non_compliant.title: Non-compliant equipment
2_5_1_non_compliant.description: Withdraw from service, label to indicate remedial action and warn against further use.

2_5_2_compliant.title: Compliant equipment tag
2_5_2_compliant.description: Fit durable, non-reusable, non-metallic tag with: (a) name of person and entity who performed test; (b) test date, retest date, reference to AS/NZS 3760. Colour coding per period. New equipment in Australia: tag with 'new to service', entry date, next test date, statement 'not tested per AS/NZS 3760'.

2_6_documentation.title: Documentation
2_6_documentation.description: Australia: records should be kept. New Zealand: records shall be kept with register of all equipment tested. Records retained for 7 years or as required by regulator.

d.title: Appendix D — Test of earthing continuity (Normative)
d.description: Measurement method: resistance between accessible earthed metal parts and earth pin of mains plug. Pass: <=1 Ohm.

e.title: Appendix E — Insulation testing (Normative)
e.description: Leakage current test or insulation resistance test methods for Class I, II, EPODs.

h.title: Appendix H — Test for operating time of RCDs (Normative)
h.description: RCD test instrument used to measure tripping time. Type I (10mA): max 40ms. Type II (30mA): max 300ms.
